retentionRate7d.definition: Percentage of users who interact with the contract again within 7 days of their first interaction
retentionRate7d.interpretation: Low D7 retention means users are not finding enough value to return. Above 40% is strong for DeFi.

retentionRate30d.definition: Percentage of users who interact again within 30 days
retentionRate30d.interpretation: D30 retention above 25% indicates a sticky product. Below 10% signals serious churn risk.

successRate.definition: Percentage of transactions that complete without reverting
successRate.interpretation: Below 85% success rate indicates UX friction, gas estimation issues, or contract bugs.

failureRate.definition: Percentage of transactions that revert or fail
failureRate.interpretation: High failure rate directly damages user trust and increases gas costs for users.

botRatio.definition: Percentage of wallets classified as bots based on behavioral patterns
botRatio.interpretation: High bot ratio inflates metrics and may indicate wash trading or MEV exploitation.

whaleConcentration.definition: Percentage of total volume controlled by top 10 wallets
whaleConcentration.interpretation: High whale concentration creates exit risk. Healthy protocols have distributed activity.

dau.definition: Daily Active Users — unique wallets interacting per day
dau.interpretation: DAU trend is more important than absolute value. 7-day declining trend is a warning signal.

mau.definition: Monthly Active Users — unique wallets interacting in the last 30 days
mau.interpretation: MAU/DAU ratio above 0.3 indicates strong daily engagement relative to monthly base.

newUsers.definition: Wallets interacting with the contract for the first time in the period
newUsers.interpretation: New user growth is the primary acquisition signal. Declining new users means marketing or product issues.

churnRate.definition: Percentage of previously active users who did not return in the current period
churnRate.interpretation: Churn above 40% monthly means the protocol is losing users faster than it acquires them.

tvl.definition: Total Value Locked — USD value of assets deposited in the contract
tvl.interpretation: TVL is the primary trust signal for DeFi. A 10%+ drop in 7 days warrants immediate investigation.

volume.definition: Total USD value of transactions processed in the period
volume.interpretation: Volume growth indicates increasing protocol utility. Compare against TVL for utilization ratio.

fees.definition: Total protocol fees collected in the period
fees.interpretation: Fee revenue is the sustainability metric. Protocols with growing fees can fund development.

gasEfficiency.definition: Average gas used per transaction relative to the contract's historical baseline
gasEfficiency.interpretation: High gas usage reduces accessibility. Optimization opportunities exist above 300k gas/tx.

avgGasUsed.definition: Mean gas units consumed per transaction
avgGasUsed.interpretation: Compare against similar protocol types. ERC20 transfers should be ~65k, complex DeFi ~200k.

liquidityUtilization.definition: Ratio of volume to TVL — how efficiently locked liquidity is being used
liquidityUtilization.interpretation: Low utilization means capital is sitting idle. High utilization (>100%) may indicate liquidity risk.

volumeToTvlRatio.definition: Daily volume divided by TVL — capital efficiency metric
volumeToTvlRatio.interpretation: A ratio above 0.1 means the protocol turns over 10%+ of its TVL daily — strong capital efficiency.

engagementScore.definition: Composite score (0-100) based on transaction frequency, value, and recency per user
engagementScore.interpretation: Engagement score below 30 means users are passive or disengaged. Target high-engagement cohorts for retention.

avgTransactionsPerUser.definition: Mean number of transactions per unique wallet in the period
avgTransactionsPerUser.interpretation: Average below 1 means most users only interact once — a strong signal of poor retention.

uniqueUsers.definition: Total unique wallet addresses that interacted with the contract
uniqueUsers.interpretation: Unique user count is the base audience metric. Growth rate matters more than absolute number.

revenuePerUser.definition: Total fee revenue divided by unique users in the period
revenuePerUser.interpretation: Revenue per user below $0.10 means the protocol is not monetizing its user base effectively.
